cards.notes: Possibly premature — explicitly taught later in Lesson 6: Going Places (1): Where Are You Going? (explicitly re-taught as a dedicated VOCABULARY entry)
A rather informal greeting used from about 10:00 A.M. until sundown
Possibly incorrect romanization — romanization reads 'kombanwa' but should be 'konbanwa' — the second ん should be 'n', not 'm'

cards.english: Excuse me. (said when entering another person's room)
cards.notes: Said when entering another person's room | Possibly premature — explicitly taught later in Lesson 7: Going Places (2): I’m Going by Shinkansen (explicitly re-taught as a dedicated VOCABULARY entry ("Excuse me."))

Said on formal occasions, e.g. leaving a meeting | Possibly premature — explicitly taught later in Lesson 6: Going Places (1): Where Are You Going? (explicitly re-taught as a dedicated VOCABULARY entry ("Good bye."))

cards.english: Good-bye. (said when leaving the office before other people)
cards.target: おさきに しつれいします。
cards.pronunciation: osakini shi tsu rei shi masu .

cards.english: Good-bye. (said when your colleague leaves the office before you)

cards.notes: Possibly premature — explicitly taught later in Lesson 2: Possession: Whose Pen Is This? (explicitly re-taught as a dedicated VOCABULARY entry ("Please (have one)."))

cards.target: ありがとうございます。
cards.notes: Possibly premature — explicitly taught later in Lesson 2: Possession: Whose Pen Is This? (explicitly re-taught as a dedicated VOCABULARY entry ("Thank you.")) | Possibly incorrect romanization — incorrectly splits 'gozaimasu' into 'gozai masu' — should be one word with no space

Polite response when thanked or when declining an offer of help/politeness | Possibly premature — explicitly taught later in Lesson 1: Meeting: Nice to Meet You (explicitly re-taught as a dedicated VOCABULARY entry ("no."))

Used to get someone's attention, e.g. calling a waiter | Possibly premature — explicitly taught later in Lesson 1: Meeting: Nice to Meet You (explicitly re-taught as a dedicated VOCABULARY entry ("Excuse me."))

Same phrase as excuse me, used here to apologize | Possibly premature — explicitly taught later in Lesson 14: Giving and Receiving: I Received It from My Friend (the closely related form すみませんでした gets its own dedicated VOCABULARY entry glossed "I'm sorry (for what I did a while ago)" — a near-exact re-teaching of this item's sense, worth the reviewer's judgment even though the surface string differs slightly)
Suffix — attaches after a person's name; cannot be used after one's own name (written 〜さん in the source)

cards.english: I look forward to working with you.
cards.target: よろしく おねがいします。
cards.notes: Usually combined with はじめまして when being introduced | Possibly incorrect romanization — onegaishimasu is incorrectly split with spaces between morphemes
Possibly incorrect romanization — should be one continuous word without internal space
From さあ、わかりません — さあ expresses hesitation before answering

cards.target: これは とけいです。
これは とけいじゃありません。
cards.pronunciation: kore wa tokei ja ari mase n .

cards.target: これは スミスさんの とけいです。
cards.pronunciation: kore wa sumisu san no tokei desu .

cards.target: これは わたしの ペンです。
cards.pronunciation: kore wa watashi no pen desu .

cards.target: これは わたしのです。

cards.notes: Possibly incorrect romanization — The word should be romanized as 'namae', not split into individual syllables as 'na ma e'
Possibly incorrect romanization — spacing breaks the auxiliary verb ます into separate syllables; should be 'onegai shimasu'
